message:
feat(stat): add strmode (BSD mode-bit-to-ls-style-string) (bd-pjego)

BSD strmode(mode_t mode, char *p) writes the 11-character `ls -l`-
style permission string into the caller's buffer plus a trailing
NUL — the format used by ls(1), find(1)'s `-printf %M`, and any
tool that wants to render mode bits human-readably. Currently
missing from frankenlibc-abi.

* core::stat::strmode_bytes(mode) → [u8; 11] does the byte-level
  formatting: file-type char + 9 permission chars (r/w/x/-) with
  setuid/setgid/sticky overlays (lowercase s/t when execute bit
  set, uppercase S/T when not), plus a trailing space for the
  ACL/extended-attribute slot (BSD convention).
* string_abi::strmode shim copies the 11 bytes + NUL into the
  caller's 12-byte buffer, with NULL-pointer guard.

New crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stat/ module (first stat.h family
member). Per CLAUDE.md no-libc rule, the POSIX S_IF*/S_I*USR
constants are hard-coded with their canonical octal values.

28 core unit tests + 8 abi integration tests covering: every file
type, every permission bit in isolation, all four (setuid+x) ×
(setuid no-x) × (setgid+x) × (setgid no-x) × (sticky+x) × (sticky
no-x) overlay states, /tmp's classic 1777, the all-special 7755 /
7644 corners, NULL-pointer no-op, and a sentinel-byte buffer-
overrun check. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
